sections.label: Section 2.4.460b
sections.translation: I must wait for my brother . The advice that he gives me about this matter , I shall follow . I ’ ll go make inquiry at the harbor , when he is to come back .

sections.label: Section 2.4.463
sections.original: At ego Antiphonem quaeram , ut quae acta hic sint sciat .
sections.translation: And I ’ ll go look for Antipho , that he may learn what has passed here . But look , I see him coming this way , just in the very nick of time .

sections.label: Section 3.1.465
sections.original: Enimvero , Antipho , multimodis cum istoc animo es vituperandus .
sections.translation: Indeed , Antipho , in many ways you are to be blamed for these feelings ; to have thus run away , and intrusted your existence to the protection of other people . Did you suppose that others would give more attention to your interests than your own self ? For , however other matters stood , certainly you should have thought of her whom you have now at home , that she might not suffer any harm in consequence of her confiding in you ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.470
sections.original: Cuius nunc miserae spes opesque sunt in te uno omnes sitae .
sections.translation: whose hopes and resources , poor thing , are all now centred in yourself alone .

sections.label: Section 3.1.471
sections.original: Equidem , here , nos iam dudum hic te absentem incusamus qui abieris .
sections.translation: Why really , master , we have for some time been censuring you here in your absence , for having thus gone away .

sections.label: Section 3.1.472
sections.translation: You are the very person I was looking for .

sections.label: Section 3.1.472b
sections.original: Sed ea causa nihilo magis defecimus .
sections.translation: But still , we were not a bit the more remiss on that account .

sections.label: Section 3.1.473
sections.original: Loquere , obsecro ; quonam in loco sunt res et fortunae meae ?
sections.translation: Tell me , I beg of you , in what posture are my interests and fortunes . Has my father any suspicion ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.474b
sections.translation: Not any at present .

sections.label: Section 3.1.474c
sections.translation: Is there still any hope ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.474d
sections.translation: I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 3.1.474e
sections.translation: Alas !

sections.label: Section 3.1.475
sections.original: Nisi Phaedria haud cessavit pro te eniti .
sections.translation: But Phaedria has not neglected to use his endeavors in your behalf .

sections.label: Section 3.1.475b
sections.translation: He did nothing new .

sections.label: Section 3.1.476
sections.original: Tur Phormio itidem in hac re , ut in aliis , strenuum hominem praebuit .
sections.translation: Then Phormio , too ; in this matter , just as in every thing else , showed himself a man of energy .

sections.label: Section 3.1.477
sections.translation: What did he do ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.477b
sections.translation: With his words he silenced the old man , who was very angry .

sections.label: Section 3.1.478
sections.translation: Well done , Phormio !

sections.label: Section 3.1.478b
sections.translation: I , too , did all I could .

sections.label: Section 3.1.478c
sections.translation: My dear Geta , I love you all .

sections.label: Section 3.1.479
sections.original: Sic habent principia sese ut dico : adhuc tranquilla res est ;
sections.translation: The commencement is just in this position , as I tell you : matters , at present are going on smoothly ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.480
sections.original: Mansurusque patruum pater est , dum huc adveniat .
sections.translation: and your father intends to wait for your uncle till he arrives .

sections.label: Section 3.1.480b
sections.translation: Why him ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.480c
sections.translation: He said he was wishful to act by his advice , in all that relates to this business .

sections.label: Section 3.1.482
sections.original: Quantus metus est mihi venire huc salvum nunc patruum , Geta .
sections.translation: How greatly now , Geta , I do dread my uncle ’ s safe arrival ! For , according to his single sentence , from what I hear , I am to live or die .

sections.label: Section 3.1.484
sections.translation: Here comes Phaedria .

sections.label: Section 3.1.484b
sections.translation: Where is he , pray ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.484c
sections.original: Eccum ab sua palaestra exit foras .
sections.translation: See , he ’ s coming from his place of exercise . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.2.485
sections.translation: Prithee , hear me , Dorio .

sections.label: Section 3.2.486b
sections.translation: I ’ ll not hear you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.486c
sections.translation: Only a moment .

sections.label: Section 3.2.486d
sections.translation: Let me alone .

sections.label: Section 3.2.487
sections.translation: Do hear what I have to say .

sections.label: Section 3.2.487b
sections.original: At enim taedet iam audire eadem millies .
sections.translation: Why really I am tired of hearing the same thing a thousand times over .

sections.label: Section 3.2.488
sections.original: At nunc dicam quod libenter audias .
sections.translation: But now , I have something to tell you that you ’ ll hear with pleasure .

sections.label: Section 3.2.488b
sections.translation: Speak then ; I ’ m listening .

sections.label: Section 3.2.489
sections.original: Nequeo te exorare ut maneas triduum hoc ? Quo nunc abis ?
sections.translation: Can I not prevail on you to wait for only three days ? Whither are you going now ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.490
sections.original: Mirabar si tu mihi quicquam afferres novi .
sections.translation: I was wondering if you had any thing new to offer .

sections.label: Section 3.2.491
sections.original: Hei , metuo lenonem ne quid — suo suat capiti .
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid for this Procurer , lest —

sections.label: Section 3.2.491b
sections.translation: Something may befall his own safety . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.2.492
sections.translation: You don ’ t believe me ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.492b
sections.translation: You guess right .

sections.label: Section 3.2.492c
sections.translation: But if I pledge my word .

sections.label: Section 3.2.492d
sections.translation: Nonsense !

sections.label: Section 3.2.493
sections.original: Foeneratum istuc beneficium tibi pulchre dices .
sections.translation: You will have reason to say that this kindness was well laid out by you on interest .

sections.label: Section 3.2.493b
sections.translation: Stuff !

sections.label: Section 3.2.494
sections.original: Crede mihi , gaudebis facto : verum hercle hoc est .
sections.translation: Believe me , you will be glad you did so ; upon my faith , it is the truth .

sections.label: Section 3.2.494b
sections.translation: Mere dreams !

sections.label: Section 3.2.495
sections.translation: Do but try ; the time is not long .

sections.label: Section 3.2.495b
sections.translation: The same story over again .

sections.label: Section 3.2.496
sections.original: Tu mihi cognatus , tu parens , tu amicus , tu —
sections.translation: You will be my kinsman , my father , my friend ; you —

sections.label: Section 3.2.496b
sections.translation: Now , do prate on .

sections.label: Section 3.2.497
sections.original: Adeone ingenio esse duro te atque inexorabili
sections.translation: For you to be of a disposition so harsh and inexorable , that neither by pity nor by entreaties can you be softened !

sections.label: Section 3.2.499
sections.original: Adeon te esse incogitantem atque impudentem , Phaedria ,
sections.translation: For you to be of a disposition so unreasonable and so unconscionable , Phaedria ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.500
sections.original: Ut phaleratis dictis ducas me , et meam ductes gratiis ?
sections.translation: that you can be talking me over with fine words , [*] and be for amusing yourself with what ’ s my property for nothing !

sections.label: Section 3.2.501
sections.translation: I am sorry for him .

sections.label: Section 3.2.501b
sections.translation: Alas ! I feel it to be too true .

sections.label: Section 3.2.501c
sections.translation: How well each keeps up to his character !

sections.label: Section 3.2.502
sections.original: Neque , Antipho alia quum occupatus esset sollicitudine ,
sections.translation: And would that this misfortune had not befallen me at a time when Antipho was occupied with other cares as well .

sections.label: Section 3.2.503b
sections.original: Ah , quid istuc autem est , Phaedria ?
sections.translation: Ah Phaedria , why , what is the matter ?
